calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-21-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Continued To Sep-18-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Henning, of the Law Offices of John Henning, Tel. 415.398.5000, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RAYANN, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Rayann fails to satisfy its initial burden of production. "Although liability might easily be found where the landowner has actual knowledge of the dangerous condition, '[t]he landowner's lack of knowledge of the dangerous condition is not a defense. He has an affirmative duty to exercise ordinary care to keep the premises in a reasonably safe condition, and therefore must inspect them or take other proper means to ascertain their condition. And if, by the exercise of reasonable care, he would have discovered the dangerous condition, he is liable.'" (Swanberg v. O'Mectin (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 325, 330 (emphasis in original); see also Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1205-1206.) Rayann did not satisfy its initial burden because it presented no evidence that a reasonable inspection of the premises would not have disclosed the dangerous condition of the property. While Rayann argued that the danger or defect was latent or not apparent, it submitted no evidence to support its argument. The trier of fact could infer otherwise based on the record on this motion. Plaintiff David Mizer's deposition testimony and the attached picture indicate that the metal surface was relatively smooth and slippery. (Mizer Depo. 25:21-26:13, 31:20-32:13; Exhibit B to Mizer Depo.) The trier of fact could conclude that Rayann should have known (i.e., had constructive knowledge) that a smooth metal plate is a dangerous condition of property. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
